intro: Noccaea cepaeifolia, also known as Sulfur Flower or Garlic Wort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its small white or purple flowers and garlic-like scent. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments, such as respiratory infections and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Noccaea cepaeifolia include glucosinolates, sulfur compounds, and polyphenols, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Noccaea cepaeifolia is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
